EMINI S&P 500 - Individual Market Analysis

CoT signals and diagnostics for EMINI S&P 500 futures.

As of 2026-03-10

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a Speculator Net % Open Interest of -6.8% and a Spec Z-score of -0.165, this setup aligns with Range-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Mean reversion, not Trend continuation. (1)
  • The signal is currently in a "Normal" synthetic state within a "Balanced_Short_Bias" market structure regime. (1)
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Stable; Threshold Proximity: Far; Revision Sensitivity: Moderate.

Methodology Applied:

  • Z-score stretch rules define extreme positioning at |z| >= 2, which indicates high fragility. (1)
  • The 4-week flow reversal metric is the primary indicator of shifts in tactical risk appetite. (1)
  • Price falling alongside negative positioning flow implies a fading of sponsorship for the prevailing trend. (1)
  • E-MINI S&P 500 CoT data (Latest: 2026-03-10). (1)

Key Dynamics:

  • The main driver is a stabilizing Speculator Z-score (-0.165), indicating a reversion from previous bearish extremes toward a neutral mean. (1)
  • Negative 4-week flow (-1.3) suggests ongoing tactical de-risking, despite the lack of extreme crowding. (1)
  • Conditional Invalidation: A Speculator Z-score expansion beyond +/- 2.0, triggering a shift into a "Crowded" regime. (1)
  • Positioning tension is low (0.52), reflecting a market structure where neither speculators nor hedgers are at extreme levels. (1)

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Range-bound consolidation as positioning returns to historical norms.
  • Upside risk: A short-covering squeeze if price stabilizes while shorts continue to reduce. (1)
  • Downside risk: Accelerated de-risking if negative flow momentum increases alongside a price break. (1)

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks); CoT signals are driven by weekly flow shifts and positioning stretch.
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; the absence of extreme positioning makes this a secondary confirmation signal.

Macro Relevance:

  • This signal informs institutional risk appetite and the warehousing of equity risk. (1)
  • The implied economic mechanism is institutional portfolio de-risking and dealer gamma hedging. (1)
  • Cycle position: Not determined.

Regime Context:

  • The "Balanced" regime has persisted for approximately four weeks, transitioning from a "Spec Long Unwind" phase. (1)
  • Direction of change: Stabilising.

Model Limitations:

  • Weekly reporting lag in CoT data may delay identification of rapid intra-week reversals. (1)
  • Commercial hedging noise can sometimes obscure pure speculative directional intent. (1)

Data & References:

  • E-MINI S&P 500 - CHICAGO MERCANTILE EXCHANGE (2026-03-10). (1)
  • Influential datapoints: Spec_zscore (-0.165), Flow_4w (-1.3), and Price (6787.25). (1)
  • VIX futures positioning and market-cap weighted price-action divergence would improve reliability.
